concepts.lesson: Inertia is the resistance of a body to change in its motion. It is a property of matter that depends on the mass of an object. The greater the mass, the more inertia an object has.
key_points: Inertia is a measure of an object's resistance to change
The amount of inertia depends on the mass of the object
More massive objects have greater inertia

concepts.lesson: A force is a push or pull that causes an object to change its motion. Forces can be contact forces, such as friction, or non-contact forces, such as gravity.
key_points: Forces are pushes or pulls that cause motion
Contact forces require physical contact between objects
Non-contact forces do not require physical contact

concepts.lesson: Momentum is the product of an object's mass and velocity. It is a measure of an object's tendency to continue moving in a straight line.
key_points: Momentum depends on both mass and velocity
Greater momentum means more resistance to change in motion
Objects with greater momentum are harder to stop

concepts.lesson: Friction is a force that opposes motion between two surfaces in contact. It depends on the type of surface and the normal force between them.
key_points: Friction is a force opposing motion
Type and normal force affect friction's magnitude
Friction can be static or kinetic

concepts.lesson: Gravity is a non-contact force that attracts objects with mass towards each other. It depends on the masses of the objects and the distance between them.
key_points: It depends on object masses and distance
Gravity always pulls objects towards each other
